question: What is the definition of the abbreviation RPV?

nuscale_ch01_0019.title: Operating Characteristics
nuscale_ch01_0019.summary: The NuScale Power Module (NPM) operates using natural circulation for reactor coolant flow, eliminating the need for reactor coolant pumps, and features a small, modular, integral pressurized water reactor design. It employs passive safety systems—including dedicated emergency core cooling and decay heat removal systems—that do not rely on electrical power, and its components are partially immersed in a reactor pool for enhanced safety. The NPM allows for flexible power maneuvering using control rods and soluble boron, and its design integrates the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within the reactor pressure vessel, minimizing external piping and penetrations.

nuscale_ch01_0117.title: Figure 1.7-3f: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0117.summary: This section provides the legend for piping and instrumentation diagrams (P&IDs) used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It lists and describes various symbols representing sample probes, pressure and temperature sensors, flow measurement devices, and other instrumentation components, ensuring standardized interpretation of the diagrams.

nuscale_ch01_0020.title: Safety Considerations
nuscale_ch01_0020.summary: The NuScale design enhances safety by maintaining a vacuum in the containment vessel (CNV), which reduces corrosion, aids in leak detection, and limits combustible gases. Its passive safety systems and integral design eliminate large-break loss-of-coolant accident (LOCA) scenarios and reduce reliance on external power, resulting in a lower core damage frequency compared to conventional reactors.

nuscale_ch01_0111.title: Figure 1.7-2: Instrumentatio n and Controls Symbol Legend
nuscale_ch01_0111.summary: This section provides a legend explaining the symbols used for instrumentation and controls in NuScale’s safety analysis diagrams. It describes the logic gate functions, control room indicators, switch types, and other symbols relevant to system monitoring and control, ensuring clear interpretation of the diagrams. Additionally, it includes notes on piping and instrumentation diagram conventions, such as vent and drain representations.

nuscale_ch01_0013.title: Proprietary Information
nuscale_ch01_0013.summary: This section explains the organization and numbering of text, tables, figures, and pages within the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR), following specific regulatory guidance. It also clarifies that while the FSAR does not contain proprietary or safeguards information, some sensitive content is withheld from public disclosure and is clearly marked, with a separate public version provided that excludes such material.

nuscale_ch01_0014.title: Acronyms and Abbreviations
nuscale_ch01_0014.summary: This section explains the conventions used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) for numbering and organizing text, tables, figures, and pages, as well as the handling of proprietary and sensitive information. It clarifies that tables and figures are numbered according to their section, pages are sequentially numbered, and sensitive content is withheld from the public version in compliance with regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0005.title: Reactor Type
nuscale_ch01_0005.summary: The NuScale reactor is a small modular pressurized water reactor (PWR) featuring a passive safety design. Each NuScale Power Module (NPM) integrates the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel, surrounded by a steel containment vessel designed to withstand high-pressure events. The plant can include up to 12 modules, each producing 160 MWt (about 50 MWe), for a total net output of approximately 570 MWe for a 12-module facility.

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the major design features and parameters of the NuScale Power Plant with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It highlights differences in aspects such as electrical output, core design, safety systems, and reactor components, using summary tables for direct comparison. The data show that NuScale's design is significantly smaller, simpler, and incorporates different safety and operational features compared to conventional PWRs.
